rows.notes: Page title / section heading at top. Handwriting is legible but the Latin/archaic spelling is slightly unclear (e.g. 'Arrearagia') so given less than perfect confidence.

rows.description: Sayer (olim Myne) Capons incl: pro Anno -
rows.notes: Numbers well aligned in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Pounds appears as '01' read as 1; shillings 8 and pence 4 are clear and within valid ranges.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Capons received from Sayer (formerly Myne) for the year, amounting to £1 8s 4d.

rows.description: Hertford College (olim Hart Hall) pro Bien:
rows.notes: Description and amounts are clear. Pound = '03' (read as 3), shillings 6 and pence 8 are crisply written and valid.
rows.english_description: Biennial payment received from Hertford College (formerly Hart Hall).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Biennial payment received from Hertford College (formerly Hart Hall), amounting to £3 6s 8d.

rows.notes: Amounts aligned and clear: 09 0 0 (read as 9 pounds, 0s, 0d). Description legible enough to identify name and 'pro an'.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from Trethevan for one year and a half, amounting to £9 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amounts (13 / 6 / 8) are clear and validate correctly. The word between 'pro' and 'Biennio' is heavily scratched out; description transcribed indicating the scribble. Slight uncertainty only in the crossed text, not the numbers.
rows.english_description: Biennial payment from Medenham (entry partly scribbled).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Biennial payment from Medenham, amounting to £13 6s 8d.

rows.notes: Clear, centered small header introducing the following group of entries ('Little Pew'). No amounts present.

Pounds (11) and shillings (19) are clear. Pence reads as '10' with a small superscript/mark interpreted as a three‑quarters symbol (¾ => 0.75). The fractional symbol is faint/ambiguous, so confidence is moderate.
rows.english_description: Biennial payment from William Taylor and his wife for the Little Pew.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Biennial payment from William Taylor and his wife for the Little Pew, amounting to £11 19s 10.75d.

rows.notes: Pounds 0 (written as '0'), shillings 15 and pence '10' are readable. As with the previous row there is a small fractional mark after the pence that appears to be ¾ (three farthings) but is faint — interpreted as 0.75 with moderate confidence.
rows.english_description: Annual payment from Tream Arundell (Minchin) for the Little Pew.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual payment from Tream Arundell (Minchin) for the Little Pew, amounting to £0 15s 10.75d.

rows.notes: A subtotal line (no left description) under the Little Pew entries. Pounds (308) and shillings (13) are legible; pence shows '6' with a small fractional mark similar to ¾ seen above — interpreted as 0.75. The fraction on this larger total is somewhat faint, so moderate confidence.
Clear handwriting and numbers: 2 pounds, 12 shillings, 2 pence. All values fall within valid ranges and align with the ruling lines.
rows.english_description: Biennial payment from Widow Ryman for the Little Pew.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Biennial payment from Widow Ryman for the Little Pew, amounting to £2 12s 2d.

rows.notes: Description is legible though the first word might be 'Thorp' or 'Thrup' — amount 12 pounds, 10 shillings, 0 pence is clearly written and validates correctly. Slight uncertainty only in the exact spelling of the first word.
rows.english_description: Annuity payment (Thrup) covering two years and a half (biennium and a half).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annuity payment (Thrup) covering two years and a half, amounting to £12 10s 0d.

rows.notes: Final total under the two small entries (Widow Ryman + Thrup Annuity). The sum (15 2 2) is clearly written, aligned and internally consistent with the two rows above (2 12 2 + 12 10 0 = 15 2 2).
